things.name: Alhambra Palace
things.description: Spain's most visited monument; the extraordinary 13th-14th century Nasrid palace complex with the Palacios Nazaries, Generalife gardens and Alcazaba fortress

things.name: Generalife Gardens
things.description: The summer palace and garden retreat of the Nasrid sultans with ornate water channels, rose gardens and panoramic views across the Alhambra and Sierra Nevada

things.name: Albaicin Quarter (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Moorish old quarter of whitewashed carmenes (villas) climbing the hillside opposite the Alhambra with labyrinthine medieval streets

things.name: Granada Cathedral and Royal Chapel
things.description: Spain's finest Renaissance cathedral (1523) with the adjacent Royal Chapel housing the tombs of Ferdinand and Isabella, unifiers of modern Spain

things.name: Sacromonte
things.description: Granada's cave district hewn into the hillside above the Albaicin; the birthplace of flamenco in its most raw form; evening zambra performances are unmissable

things.name: Banuelo Arab Baths
things.description: The best-preserved Moorish bathhouse in Spain, dating from the 11th century; star-shaped skylights create pools of light over the vaulted stone chambers

things.name: Science Park (Parque de las Ciencias)
things.description: Andalusia's largest interactive science museum with a planetarium, butterfly house, observatory and extensive hands-on exhibits for all ages

things.name: Sierra Nevada National Park
things.description: Europe's southernmost ski resort and Spain's highest mountain range just 30km from Granada; hiking, skiing and the Veleta summit at 3,396m

things.name: Mirador de San Nicolas
things.description: The most celebrated viewpoint in Granada, offering the classic postcard view of the Alhambra framed by the Sierra Nevada at sunset

things.name: Estadio Nuevo Los Carmenes
things.description: Home of Granada CF with 19,336 capacity; the club's remarkable story included reaching the UEFA Europa League quarter-finals in 2020-21
